dev.jeka.core.api.function

Class JkRunnables

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.api.function.JkRunnables
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Runnable

    public class JkRunnables
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Runnable
    A mutable container for Runnable. From this object you can replace the underlying Runnable or chain it with other ones.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Static Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      JkRunnables append(java.lang.Runnable... runnables)
      Adds the specified Runnables at the end of the execution chain.
      JkRunnables append(java.lang.String name, java.lang.Runnable runnable)
      Adds the specified Runnable at the end of the execution chain.
      JkRunnables appendIf(boolean condition, java.lang.String name, java.lang.Runnable runnable)
      Same as append(String, Runnable) but effective only the specified condition
      boolean contains(java.lang.String runnableName)
      Returns true if the execution chain contains a runnable with the specified name.
      java.lang.Runnable getRunnable(int index)
      Retrieves the Runnable stored at the specified index in the execution chain.
      java.util.List<java.lang.String> getRunnableNames()
      Returns the name of the Runnables, in the order of execution chain.
      int getSize()
      Returns the total number of entries in the execution chain.
      JkRunnables insertAfter(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String afterRunnableName, java.lang.Runnable runnable)
      Inserts the specified runnable in the execution chain, just after another one.
      JkRunnables insertBefore(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String beforeRunnableName, java.lang.Runnable runnable)
      Inserts the specified runnable in the execution chain, just before another one.
      static JkRunnables of()
      Creates a JkRunnables delegating to a no-op runnable.
      JkRunnables remove(java.lang.String runnableName)
      Removes the runnable having the specified name, from the execution list.
      JkRunnables replaceOrAppend(java.lang.String name, java.lang.Runnable runnable)
      Replaces the specified Runnable with the same name in the execution chain, or appends it if not found.
      JkRunnables replaceOrInsertBefore(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String beforeRunnableName, java.lang.Runnable runnable)
      Replaces a Runnable with the same name in the execution chain if exists, otherwise inserts it before a specified runnable.
      void run()
      Executes the Runnables, in the order of the execution chain.
      JkRunnables setLogTasks(boolean log)
      Sets if each runnable execution should be wrapped in a log task.
      JkRunnables setTaskSuffix(java.lang.String suffix)
      Sets the displayed suffix for the task.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Method Detail

      • of

        public static JkRunnables of()
        Creates a JkRunnables delegating to a no-op runnable.

      • append

        public JkRunnables append(java.lang.String name,
                          java.lang.Runnable runnable)
        Adds the specified Runnable at the end of the execution chain.
        Parameters:
        name - The name referencing the runnable. The name can be used to remove the runnable form the chain or add others with place relative to this one.
        See Also:
        remove(String), insertBefore(String, String, Runnable), insertAfter(String, String, Runnable), contains(String)

      • append

        public JkRunnables append(java.lang.Runnable... runnables)
        Adds the specified Runnables at the end of the execution chain.

      • appendIf

        public JkRunnables appendIf(boolean condition,
                            java.lang.String name,
                            java.lang.Runnable runnable)
        Same as append(String, Runnable) but effective only the specified condition

      • insertBefore

        public JkRunnables insertBefore(java.lang.String name,
                                java.lang.String beforeRunnableName,
                                java.lang.Runnable runnable)
        Inserts the specified runnable in the execution chain, just before another one.
        Parameters:
        name - The name of the runnable to insert.
        beforeRunnableName - The name of the runnable that we must insert before.
        runnable - The runnable to insert.

      • replaceOrInsertBefore

        public JkRunnables replaceOrInsertBefore(java.lang.String name,
                                         java.lang.String beforeRunnableName,
                                         java.lang.Runnable runnable)
        Replaces a Runnable with the same name in the execution chain if exists, otherwise inserts it before a specified runnable.
        Parameters:
        name - The name referencing the runnable.
        beforeRunnableName - The name of the runnable that the new runnable should be inserted before.
        runnable - The runnable to replace or insert.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if the Runnable is null.

      • insertAfter

        public JkRunnables insertAfter(java.lang.String name,
                               java.lang.String afterRunnableName,
                               java.lang.Runnable runnable)
        Inserts the specified runnable in the execution chain, just after another one.
        Parameters:
        name - The name of the runnable to insert.
        afterRunnableName - The name of the runnable that we must insert after.
        runnable - The runnable to insert.

      • remove

        public JkRunnables remove(java.lang.String runnableName)
        Removes the runnable having the specified name, from the execution list.
        Parameters:
        runnableName - The name of the runnable to remove.

      • replaceOrAppend

        public JkRunnables replaceOrAppend(java.lang.String name,
                                   java.lang.Runnable runnable)
        Replaces the specified Runnable with the same name in the execution chain, or appends it if not found.
        Parameters:
        name - The name referencing the runnable.
        runnable - The Runnable to replace or append.
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - If the Runnable is null.

      • getRunnableNames

        public java.util.List<java.lang.String> getRunnableNames()
        Returns the name of the Runnables, in the order of execution chain.

      • contains

        public boolean contains(java.lang.String runnableName)
        Returns true if the execution chain contains a runnable with the specified name.

      • setLogTasks

        public JkRunnables setLogTasks(boolean log)
        Sets if each runnable execution should be wrapped in a log task.

      • setTaskSuffix

        public JkRunnables setTaskSuffix(java.lang.String suffix)
        Sets the displayed suffix for the task.

      • getSize

        public int getSize()
        Returns the total number of entries in the execution chain.

      • getRunnable

        public java.lang.Runnable getRunnable(int index)
        Retrieves the Runnable stored at the specified index in the execution chain.

      • run

        public void run()
        Executes the Runnables, in the order of the execution chain.
        Specified by:
        run in interface java.lang.Runnable
